chief_complaint: 42-year-old woman presented with acute coronary syndrome and acute pulmonary edema.
history: The patient presented with ST-segment elevation in aVR with reciprocal changes on electrocardiography and inferoposterior wall hypokinesia with reduced ejection fraction (40%) on echocardiography. She was urgently transferred for coronary angiography (CAG).
exam: Vital signs: not available. Physical examination: not available.
labs: Electrocardiography: ST-segment elevation in aVR with reciprocal changes. Echocardiography: inferoposterior wall hypokinesia with reduced ejection fraction (40%).
correct_diagnosis: Acute Coronary Syndrome with Severe Peripheral Arterial Disease
differentials: Acute Coronary Syndrome with Severe Peripheral Arterial Disease
correct_ranking: Acute Coronary Syndrome with Severe Peripheral Arterial Disease
